Quick thought from this morning while reading the Brunetti et al. ApJ paper on Type Ia supernova progenitors. They argue the bimodality in light-curve decline rates is best explained by two distinct progenitor channels — a sub-Chandrasekhar double-detonation pathway producing the fast-decliners and the canonical Chandrasekhar-mass deflagration producing the slow-decliners. The mass-stretch relation falls out naturally if you assume the burning efficiency scales with the WD core density at ignition. Worth checking whether their Bayesian model selection actually distinguishes this from a simple metallicity-dependent normalisation.
